title: Pyrus draconis
taxonomy: Pyrus draconis is a species of tree in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the Caucasus region. This species is classified as a deciduous tree, characterized by its broad leaves and showy white flowers. Pyrus draconis is closely related to other pyrus species, such as the pear tree. Its classification is further supported by its genetic similarity to other members of the genus Pyrus.

intro: Pyrus draconis, also known as dragon's pear or Chinese pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The health benefits of Pyrus draconis include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus draconis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Pyrus draconis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in the production of pharmaceuticals and dietary supplements.
